content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 25, 2002


Eric F. Galen
Paul, Hastings, Janofsky & Walker, LLP
515 South Flower Street, 25th Floor
Los Angeles, CA 90071-2228

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. W-02-241

Dear Mr. Galen:

     This letter is verification of the receipt of your August 22, 2002 confirmation
request on the ability of a local health care district, organized under California Health &
Safety Code section 32000 et seq., to require every candidate for its board of directors to
complete and file a statement of economic interests upon declaring candidacy. The Fair
Political Practices Commission does not provide written confirmation of telephone
advice. Additionally, under the provisions of California Code of Regulations
18329(b)(8)(B), unless you are an authorized representative of the unnamed health care
district for which you seek assistance, the Commission cannot address your questions or
provide analysis. I enclose a copy of regulation 18329 for your review.

     Should you have any future questions, please feel free to contact the Commission
at (916) 322-5660.

                                                Very truly yours,

                                                Luisa Menchaca
                                                General Counsel

                                                John W. Wallace
                                                Assistant General Counsel
                                                Legal Division
Enclosure
JWW:jg

sections.question_synthetic: Can a local health care district organized under California Health & Safety Code section 32000 et seq. require candidates for its board of directors to complete and file a statement of economic interests upon declaring candidacy?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice on this matter, citing two reasons: (1) the Commission does not provide written confirmation of telephone advice, and (2) absent authorization from the unnamed health care district itself, the Commission cannot address the questions or provide analysis under California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter, not a full advice letter. Document lacks traditional QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. The question and conclusion_synthetic are synthesized from the letter's body text explaining the FPPC's reasons for declining to respond. Confidence slightly reduced due to absence of standard Q/C formatting.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can a local health care district organized under California Health & Safety Code section 32000 et seq. require candidates for its board of directors to complete and file a statement of economic interests upon declaring candidacy?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide written advice on this matter, citing two reasons: (1) the Commission does not provide written confirmation of telephone advice, and (2) absent authorization from the unnamed health care district itself, the Commission cannot address the questions or provide analysis under California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B).

Galen Paul, Hastings, Janofsky & Walker, LLP 515 South Flower Street, 25th Floor Los Angeles, CA 90071-2228 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. W-02-241 Dear Mr. Galen: This letter is verification of the receipt of your August 22, 2002 confirmation request on the ability of a local health care district, organized under California Health & Safety Code section 32000 et seq., to require every candidate for its board of directors to complete and file a statement of economic interests upon declaring candidacy. The Fair Political Practices Commission does not provide written confirmation of telephone advice. Additionally, under the provisions of California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B), unless you are an authorized representative of the unnamed health care district for which you seek assistance, the Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. I enclose a copy of regulation 18329 for your review. Should you have any future questions, please feel free to contact the Commission at (916) 322-5660. Very truly yours, Luisa Menchaca General Counsel John W. Wallace Assistant General Counsel Legal Division Enclosure JWW:jg
embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide written advice regarding whether a local health care district may require candidates to file statements of economic interests, citing lack of authorization from the unnamed district and policy against written confirmation of telephone advice.
